content.problemStatement:
**This is worth 100100100 points in Division 111 and 505050 points in Divisions 222, 333 and 444.**

You have a tree with NNN nodes numbered 111 to NNN.

You want to assign each node a colour CiC_iCi​ (1≤Ci≤N1 \le C_i \le N1≤Ci​≤N) such that the following conditions hold:


Each colour XXX appears either 000 times or ≥2\ge 2≥2 times.
Consider all possible pairs (A,B)(A, B)(A,B) such that 1≤A<B≤N1 \le A < B \le N1≤A<B≤N and CA=CBC_A = C_BCA​=CB​:

Each edge should lie on the unique shortest path of **exactly** one such pair.


Let us call a tree **good** if it is possible to assign colours satisfying the above property.

We start with a tree of 111 node only, namely 111.

Then for each i=2...Ni = 2...Ni=2...N, do the following:


add the node iii to the tree, by making an edge between iii and PiP_iPi​.
output whether the current tree is **good** or not.
Further, if the final tree after adding all NNN nodes is **good**, we require you to output an appropriate colour scheme for that tree.

If there are multiple valid colour schemes, you should **minimize** the number of distinct colours used. If there are still multiple valid schemes, all will be accepted.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the number of nodes in the tree.
The second line contains N−1N - 1N−1 integers - P2,P3,...,PNP_2, P_3, ..., P_NP2​,P3​,...,PN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output the following:



on the first line, output a binary string SSS of length N−1N - 1N−1; where Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 if and only if the tree formed by the first (i+1)(i + 1)(i+1) nodes is **good**, otherwise Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0.
If SN−1=1S_{N - 1} = 1SN−1​=1, then output a second line containing NNN integers, C1,C2,...,CNC_1, C_2, ..., C_NC1​,C2​,...,CN​ (1≤Ci≤N1 \le C_i \le N1≤Ci​≤N) a valid colouring scheme for the final tree with the **minimum** number of distinct colours.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The tree with 222 nodes and (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) being the only edge is good, as depicted by the colouring C=[2,2]C = [2, 2]C=[2,2]. There is only one edge and one pair, and the edge lies on the shortest path of that pair.
Test Case 2 : It can be shown that the whole tree of 444 nodes is not good. For example, C=[1,2,1,2]C = [1, 2, 1, 2]C=[1,2,1,2] makes the edge (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) occur on the shortest paths of 222 different pairs.
Test Case 3 : The whole tree of 444 nodes is good as shown by the colouring C=[1,3,1,3]C = [1, 3, 1, 3]C=[1,3,1,3].
